texto: And strange were the tales of the pond in the meadow,And eager we listened with eyes opened wideTo Those tales often told by poor Mary the widow,Who lived in a cottage the meadow beside.Play not, my dear boys, near the pond in the meadow,The mermaid is waiting to pull you beneath;Climb not for a bird’s nest, the bough it may sliver,And the mermaid will drag you to darkness and death.
explicacao: Citação de J.R. Withers sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “And strange were the tales of the pond in the meadow,And eager we listened with eyes opened wideT…” articula vida, mermaids, moon-stealers em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como luto e reflexao-noturna.
